title: AI engine redesign: the city bridge (claude -p -> city agents, files -> beads)
description: Approved design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-ai-engine-city-bridge-design.md. Go supervisor slims to Watchkeeper (detectors, rate caps, backoff, kill switch, mail+nudge); captain becomes standing city agent (fable) with beads-only memory + rollover ritual; full standing crew: Shipwright (opus, 3-tier bug/improvement/feature pipeline behind unchanged Go gate), Trade Analyst, Fleet Architect (opus); consult protocol with mandatory adversarial pass >25% treasury spends; self-modification of safety rails always Tier 3 (Admiral sign-off). Migration in 5 shippable steps, fleet startable throughout. Next: writing-plans.
notes:
Implementation plan written+committed: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-06-ai-engine-city-bridge.md. 12 TDD tasks: (1) events ack/list CLI, (2) captain-gate CLI wrapping unchanged RunGate/SquashMerge, (3) CityGateway/BeadsClient exec adapters, (4) bridge config fields, (5) wake path mail+nudge+renudge+escalate, (6) respawn+orphan requeue, (7) captain-migrate files->beads (dry-run default), (8) captain template, (9) CUTOVER (migration --apply, archive state, delete ClaudeRunner/snapshot/metareview), (10) shipwright agent + fixer driver deletion, (11) analyst+architect templates (answer-nudge protocol), (12) dashboard repoint + doc banners. Recon findings baked in: gc session nudge/submit/reset/handoff EXIST (assumption 1 verified); fixer already has fix/feature/automation kinds + per-day caps; RunGate/SquashMerge clean seams. Tasks 1-4 parallelizable; 9 and 10.3 want Admiral present; rollback = engine_mode:legacy pre-cutover. Execution NOT started - pool resets Jul 8.
Admiral: implement now, OPUS ONLY (no fable, even review). Launching workflow: wave 1 = tasks 1-4 parallel, then 5,6,7,8 sequential; STOPS before task 9 (cutover) + 10 smoke - those are attended, Admiral-present steps. Tasks 11-12 templates/dashboard also included where unattended-safe (11 yes, 12 yes; 9-10 held).
Tasks 1-8, 11, 12 BUILT + COMMITTED (2 commits: waves 1-2 Go side 19+2 files; wave 3 templates+dashboard 10 files). 23 opus agents, 0 errors, gate green first pass, legacy engine intact (NewClaudeRunner present, engine_mode=legacy default). Review caught+repaired a real bd flag error in task 3 (singular --type). Reality adjustments recorded: gc mail positional args, session State field, bd owner field, decisions.jsonl action/outcome keys, L<N> lesson delimiter, acd prime cwd-discovery quirk (harmless - acd run uses real templates). Stray smoke binary gobot/captain removed; no stray commits (reflog verified). REMAINING - ATTENDED: task 9 cutover (migrate --apply, archive state, delete session/snapshot/metareview, flip engine_mode) + task 10 shipwright (template exists in plan, agent + fixer-driver deletion + synthetic-bead E2E smoke). Recommend running 9+10 with Admiral present, fresh pool (resets Jul 8). Minor residue: probe* stub entries in dolt agent registries (no gc agent remove cmd); dashboard session_state()/logentry legacy refs degrade gracefully.
close_reason: COMPLETE: all 12 plan tasks built+committed. claude -p deleted; captain/shipwright/analysts/surveyor standing agents; watchkeeper (wake+respawn+detectors+reset+surveyor cadence); state migrated to sp- beads; dashboard repointed; templates fable-audited. The two attended smokes (wake, shipwright E2E) fold into st-wm7 bring-up.

title: Post-reset bring-up: new-universe readiness (GATED on Admiral order)
description:
NEXT SESSION'S JOB (rewritten 2026-07-06 end-of-dry-dock; original checklist superseded - the machinery it asked for is now BUILT). Sequence, Admiral present throughout:

PHASE A - TORWIND era-close (runbook: docs/runbooks/universe-reset.md; spec section 5-6):
1. pg_dump safety snapshot (runbook phase 1 command).
2. spacetraders universe close --era torwind --confirm torwind (needs the torwind era row backfilled first - runbook covers it; verify with universe status).
3. Beads era-close: go run ./cmd/era-close (DRY-RUN first, review plan, then --apply) - era-tags ~216 decisions, closes ~50 open ones, demotes strategy bead sp-s2q.
4. MEMORY GATE: era-close prints the KEEP/REWRITE/RETIRE proposal table for the 62 memories - ADMIRAL APPROVES each class, apply manually per printed instructions (tooling never executes memory actions).
5. Retrospective bead from history summary --era torwind + demoted strategy.

PHASE B - universe 2:
6. ST_ACCOUNT_TOKEN env + spacetraders player register --new --agent <SYM> --faction <F> (Admiral-only; refuses while an era is open - hence phase A first). config set-player <new-id>.
7. Seed fresh strategy bead (-l strategy,era:<new>) from retro + history priors.

PHASE C - bring-up + smokes:
8. Wake smoke: acd run captain (detach) or let watchkeeper spawn; remove DISABLED briefly; go run ./cmd/captain --once; verify mail+nudge arrive, captain acks, captain events list empties. Re-set DISABLED until step 10.
9. Shipwright E2E smoke: real bead (suggest sp-s7b7 ReleaseAllActive fix - small, real, tests the whole claim->worktree->TDD->captain-gate->merge->close loop).
10. Services up (launchd plists), Admiral clears captain/DISABLED - fleet flies era 2.

CONTEXT: engine fully rebuilt (st-fx8 closed), reset machinery built (sp-4uyh closed), meta-loop live (surveyor nudged weekly once watchkeeper runs). Kill switch SET. Pool reset Jul 8.
notes: Registration itself is the Admiral's call. This bead is the checklist, not authorization.

title: Correctness wave: gate-blocker fix + confirmed bugs + dead-code prep (workflow 3)
description: Admiral-ordered continuation. Scope: st-nvl supply-filter rewire (captain report implemented directly, report status updated to prevent double work), nil-guards #28/#29, rate-limiter DI st-0tw.1, counter/param hygiene #17/#22, factory coordinator #24/#25 (fix-if-confined else captain report), storage capacity #13, fuel staleness #12, plus dead-code deletes st-0tw.2/st-0tw.3 as prep. All fixes TDD (failing test first - behavior changes), cross-model adversarial review, full gate.
notes: Universe reset mid-wave: all 8 tasks remain valid (pure code fixes, hermetic tests). The construction-sourcing task now fixes a future-universe defect rather than a live blocker - urgency down, correctness value unchanged.
close_reason: Wave complete: 8 fixed, 1 not-a-bug (#24), 1 deferred w/ report (#22), 2 dead-code deletions, 13 tests, gate green first pass, committed

title: Construction planner supply-filter defect (universe-independent; was: jump gate X1-PZ28-I67 blocker)
description: Third sequential blocker on gate X1-PZ28-I67 (THE mission). After 73f3f08 (execution wiring) + f7d644d (re-plan), planning now aborts: FindExportMarketWithGoodSupply demands HIGH/ABUNDANT supply; ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY only exporter (D45) is MODERATE. All-or-nothing planning discards the whole bill over one material. Root-caused in captain/reports/bugs/2026-07-05-construction-depth3-supply-filter-too-strict.md (status: new): planner wired to wrong locator; MODERATE-tolerant FindExportMarketBySupplyPriority already exists. Needs captain running to work its pipeline; fleet currently stopped by Admiral.
notes:
Report file is UNTRACKED in git - could be lost on clean checkout. L1-L4 refactor (st-dg6) will drift its line numbers; exported names in the two target files were frozen to keep it actionable. Treasury 7.7M vs ~1.59M bill - economics trivially fine.
UNIVERSE RESET 2026-07-05: the specific gate/agent are gone, but this code defect survives - any future construction in the new universe hits the same HIGH/ABUNDANT-only filter. Fix in flight via st-eki (workflow 3 task construction-sourcing). Close when that lands.
close_reason: Fixed in correctness wave: planner rewired to MODERATE-tolerant locator, TDD-pinned, gate green, captain report marked merged

description: Admiral ordered full-codebase quality pass. Workflow wf_5b379ccb-7ae: 13 path-disjoint partitions (~72k LOC), nw-software-crafter agents (fable x4 intricate, opus x9 mechanical), cross-model adversarial review, conditional fixes, full build+vet+test+gofmt gate. Workflow wf_5b379ccb-7ae: 32 agents, 0 errors, ~923k subagent tokens, 11min. Cross-model review found 4-partition fixes. Full extracts: ~/.claude/jobs/7ab66aae/tmp/l1l4-bugs.txt (31 suspected bugs, NOT fixed - behavior preserved) and l1l4-skips.txt (76 L5-L6 seeds). Frozen-API files honored.

title: Structural round in 3 parts (lean protocol)
description: Admiral: execute in parts, not one workflow. Part 1: API lane (ship-DTO consolidation -> retry-policy extraction, internal/adapters/api). Part 2: spawn/daemon lane (WorkerSpawner -> command-factory single-source w/ st-anu fix -> DaemonClient collapse). Part 3: mfg lane (value objects -> SupplyMonitor split). Each part: premise re-check -> pinning tests -> refactor -> cross-model review -> gate -> COMMIT + Admiral checkpoint before next part. Deliberate behavior changes only as explicit TDD steps.
notes:
Part 1 launched. Parts 2-3 held for checkpoint.
Part 1 (API lane) COMMITTED. ship-dto: premise confirmed, 3 divergent mappers -> one shipDTO+toShipData, divergence healed (explicit TDD), panic->error on malformed JSON; REVIEW CAUGHT dropped missing-section validation - repaired with failing-first tests (protocol win). retry-policy: premise adjusted, client.go 1614->1347, retry_policy.go owns classify+doWithRetry, requestWithErrorParsing gains retry+metrics (explicit), [DEBUG] printfs deleted, 27 pkg tests green, review clean. Gate green first pass, 6 agents ~356k tokens. HOLDING at checkpoint: Part 2 (spawn/daemon w/ st-anu fix) awaits Admiral nod.
Admiral nod received - Part 2 (spawn/daemon lane) launching: WorkerSpawner saga (opus) -> command-factory single-source w/ st-anu contract-worker recovery fix (fable) -> DaemonClient collapse (fable). Sequential (file collisions), cross-model reviews, full gate.
Part 3 (mfg lane) launched IN PARALLEL with part 2 per Admiral - file territories verified disjoint (incl. NewSupplyMonitor sole caller inside lane). Guards: crafters never touch out-of-scope red (retry 3x then report), reviewers diff lane paths only, part 3 gate is LANE-SCOPED; full-repo gate + commits by harbormaster after BOTH land. Part 3 items: value-objects (fable, ZERO deliberate changes - contradictory tables become named policies, outputs bit-identical) -> supply-monitor split (fable, delegation-first, public API stable).
Part 2 LANDED + COMMITTED (18 files, +1775/-697, 218 tests green, 0 repairs). worker-spawner: 12 rollback pins, no divergence found, two per-package helpers (justified vs shared pkg). command-factory: ContainerSpec+configReader single source, 30+ pins incl first-ever recovery tests, 2 declared drift heals (ContainerID, gas Force/DryRun). daemon-client: generic Persist/Start, sqlite-pinned. st-anu CORRECTED: daemon side was already fixed (9b1e982), now pinned; residual = fleet coordinator ship-reclaim, bead re-scoped. Part 3 still at sea.
close_reason: All 3 parts landed+committed, all 7 v4 structural items executed (2 with corrected premises), full-repo gate green after parallel lanes joined

title: DaemonServer.getAPIClient() news up a fresh client, bypassing the shared rate limiter and metri
description:
Level L6 · effort S · analyzer value 5 · judge-adjusted 3.5 (2/2 real, 2/2 behavior-safe)

FILES: internal/adapters/grpc/container_ops_construction.go, internal/adapters/grpc/daemon_server.go, cmd/spacetraders-daemon/main.go

PROBLEM: getAPIClient() (container_ops_construction.go:162-164) returns api.NewSpaceTradersClient() — a brand-new client with its OWN token-bucket rate limiter and NO metrics collector — called 3x within a single construction flow. These ad-hoc clients do not share the global SpaceTraders request budget, so construction traffic (the jump-gate delivery path) can push the account past the 429 threshold the rest of the daemon carefully throttles against, and their calls are invisible to Prometheus.

PROPOSAL: Inject a single api.APIClient (with rate limiter + metrics) into DaemonServer once (it already receives collaborators in NewDaemonServer, daemon_server.go:90) and have getAPIClient() return that shared instance. First step: add an apiClient field to DaemonServer, wire it from main.go's existing api.NewSpaceTradersClient() (main.go:142), and return s.apiClient.

TEST PREREQ: Assert construction ops route through the injected client (a test double), verifying a single shared rate limiter — cheap once the field exists.

JUDGE NOTES (condensed): Rate-limiter half of the problem is real and verified: getAPIClient() (container_ops_construction.go:162-164) news up api.NewSpaceTradersClient(), each of which builds its own rate.NewLimiter(2,30) (client.go:85), while main.go threads a single shared apiClient (main.go:142) as the daemon's throttled budget. Construction thus opens independent token buckets outside that budget — genuine bypass on the priority gate path, and the fix (inject shared client, return s.apiClient) eliminates rather than relocates it. However the metrics half is FALSE: getMetricsCollector() (client.go:106-118) falls back to metrics.GetGlobalAPICollector(), set at daemon_server.go:223, so these clients already report to Prometheus. behavior_safe: rate.Limiter is goroutine-safe; adding one field + one NewDaemonServer param (single call site main.go:398) and returning it is a reversible injection whose only runtime change is the intended throttling — the test-double prereq is cheap. Value trimmed to 3: the concern is real and mission-relevant but overstated (false metrics claim) and bounded in impact — construction clients are minted per-RPC, not high-frequency pollers, so excess request pressure is intermitt
close_reason: Closed

title: L5-L6 refactoring backlog (adversarially judged 2026-07-05)
description: Output of the L5-L6 discovery workflow (wf_358f5138-b8d): 6 area analyzers -> synthesis -> 2-model adversarial judging of top 12 -> 7 KEPT (children of this epic), 5 REJECTED with line-level refutations, 28 below-the-line unjudged. Full evidence: docs/refactoring/2026-07-05-l5l6-discovery.json. Rejected highlights: FindExportMarket* family collapse (premise materially wrong - only 2 of 5 share shape; gate fix st-nvl stays the simple rewire); Reconciler abstraction (five bodies share nothing but a verb, one lives in cmd/captain binary - concrete ContractWorkerReconciler in st-anu remains right); depth-planning strategy (traced: no drift exists); DualTaskQueue OCP (real dup BUT bundled a behavior change to the worker-allocation gate - resubmit as dedup-only); cargo/fuel error swallowing (cargo half unreachable-defensive; fuel half real but weak).

title: Triage 31 suspected bugs surfaced by L1-L4 refactor audit
description: Crafters recorded (not fixed) 31 suspected defects during the behavior-preserving pass. Full list: ~/.claude/jobs/7ab66aae/tmp/l1l4-bugs.txt (also in workflow journal). Top real-impact candidates: (29) createMarketAcquireDeliverTask nil-panic when FindExportMarketWithGoodSupply returns (nil,nil) - SAME code path as gate blocker st-nvl, fix together; (28) FindBestExportMarket same nil-check gap; (25) run_factory_coordinator produceNodeOnly re-produces inputs inline in parallel mode - potential double purchasing; (17) AssignmentTracker.Untrack never decrements tasksByType - counters grow until restart, skewing WorkerReservationPolicy; (24) factory delivery sales may not be ledger-linked (op context never forwarded); (13) StorageShip.ConfirmDeposit can breach capacity invariant; (11/12) swallowed validation errors leave nil/stale cargo+fuel; (22) AddActivePipeline ignores its id param. Rest are stubs/dead-code/doc-drift - fold into cleanup.
notes:
Captain fix pipeline is the fixer; these came from harbormaster audit. Feed the top ones as captain bug reports when fleet resumes.
L5-L6 judge evidence adjusts two entries: bug #11 (NewCargo swallowed error) judged UNREACHABLE defensive code - ReceiveCargo/RemoveCargo pre-guards provably satisfy NewCargo invariants; bug #30 (depth-planning tautology) judged NO real drift - all depth branches traced equivalent. Bug #12 (NewFuel stale fuel) remains real but low-severity. Prioritize #29/#28 nil-panics, #25 double-purchase, #17 counter growth, #24 ledger linkage.
TRIAGE COMPLETE (Admiral asked: fix the rest?). Verdict: no blanket wave. In flight via st-eki: #12,13,17,22,24,25,28,29. Not-bugs per judges: #11,30. Already handled: #1,18,26. STANDALONE FIXES for cleanup batch (queue step 2, after workflow 3): #23 missing CoordinatorID, #16 Fuel.Consume clamp, #14 FailRoute swallowed error, #3 dead arrivalTime field, #19 dead ctor params, #15 INVESTIGATE-ONLY (asymmetric threshold may be intentional). FOLD INTO ROOT-CAUSE ITEMS: #4->st-0tw.6, #5/6->HealthMonitor delete-or-implement, #20->value-tables consolidation, #2/21/31->logging sweep, #7->getter normalization, #9->coordinator-params (serialization compat risk), #8/10->comment corrections in cleanup pass.
Wave outcomes: #24 EXONERATED (context-based linkage works; regression-pinned). #22 deferred with captain report (cross-package) - now folded into cleanup batch. Fixed+committed: #12,13,17,25,28,29 + gate rewire + st-0tw.1.
Cleanup batch landed+committed: #23,22,19,27,4,3,16,14,8,10 all resolved. #15 VERDICT: intentional safety semantics (commit e75752b archaeology) - NOT a bug, but guarding test died with predecessor repo; flight-mode threshold semantics currently unprotected -> characterization test queued into structural round. NavigateShip int ArrivalTime chain is write-only downstream (route_executor reads only ArrivalTimeStr) - future cross-package delete candidate. Remaining open from the 31: only fold-ins riding with structural round items (HealthMonitor #5/6, logging sweep #2/21/31, value tables #20, getters #7, coordinator params #9).
#15 flight-mode threshold now PROTECTED: TestSelectOptimalFlightModeExactThresholdAsymmetry (7 boundary subtests) written in phase C - the last actionable item from the original 31 that wasn't riding elsewhere. Remaining fold-ins (HealthMonitor #5/6, logging sweep #2/21/31, getters #7, coordinator params #9) live in the below-line backlog (docs/refactoring JSON).
close_reason: Triage exhausted: all 31 items resolved, exonerated, pinned, or filed - fixes across correctness wave/cleanup batch/phase C; #15 protected by test; judges killed #11/#30; remaining fold-ins (#5/6 HealthMonitor, #2/21/31 logging sweep, #7 getters, #9 coordinator params) live in the below-line backlog (docs/refactoring/2026-07-05-l5l6-discovery.json) for future rounds.

title: Visualizer: cinematic observatory (Deep Space Noir)
description: Approved design: visualizer becomes a 24/7 cinematic observatory. Deep Space Noir art direction; ship-life interpolation + cinematic camera + event drama (NO orbital motion - waypoints fixed); Theater+HUD chrome with cinema toggle; layered compositing (backdrop + upgraded Konva + DOM glass HUD), no renderer rewrite. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-05-visualizer-cinematic-observatory-design.md. Next: writing-plans -> implementation. Buildable with fleet stopped via demo/mock mode.
notes:
Decided with Admiral via visual companion (style boards). Verify at planning: nav route timestamps reach web app; event stream + gate progress endpoints exist in server/.
Implementation plan written+committed: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-05-visualizer-cinematic-observatory.md. 12 TDD tasks: theme tokens, backdrop+parallax, camera math+hook, trails, engine glow/noir restyle, server events+construction endpoints (visualizer/server ONLY - gobot untouchable during refactor), polling+connection health, event FX, glass HUD+theater+cinema toggle, demo mode, always-on hardening+soak. Discovery: motion core (transit interpolation, rotation, smoothing) already exists in domain/ship.ts + SpaceMap - plan polishes, does not rebuild. Assumptions resolved: nav timestamps YES client-side; events/gate need 2 new Express routes vs captain_events/construction tables.
Admiral deferred implementation (2026-07-05). Spec + 12-task plan committed and ready; resume via superpowers:subagent-driven-development or executing-plans against docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-05-visualizer-cinematic-observatory.md
Post-bring-up work: build when there is a live fleet to watch. Plan committed and ready (12 tasks).

title: Fleet-balance review (d-95): 5th hauler vs rebalancing
description: Fleet drifted: mfg claimed haulers, contracts down to one ship (TORWIND-3). Held pre-shutdown because total $/h stayed ~150k/hr. Captain deferred to d-95 review (~Jul 6): if $/h sags on resume, weigh buying 5th hauler vs rebalancing mfg->contracts. Decision belongs to captain+Admiral once fleet is back up.
close_reason: Obsolete: universe reset wiped the fleet - no haulers, no d-95 review. Fleet-composition decisions restart from zero in the new universe.

title: Generalize restart-orphan reconciliation to worker containers
description: RE-SCOPED after structural part 2 (2026-07-06): the daemon-side classification bug is GONE - contract workers with a coordinator/parent already reach markWorkerInterrupted (fixed by 9b1e982, predates this session) and that outcome is now locked by 4 pin tests in recover_running_containers_pin_test.go. THE RESIDUAL BUG is application-layer: the fleet coordinator never RECLAIMS ships still assigned to interrupted workers, so after a restart it loops 'no ships available' - this is the actual root cause of the session-89 observation. Fix belongs in run_fleet_coordinator.go (+ possibly assignment reconciliation on coordinator startup): on start/resume, detect FAILED/worker_interrupted child containers and release/reclaim their ship assignments. Original generic-Reconciler framing was judged fabricated (0/2); keep this concrete.
notes:
L5-L6 judges REJECTED (0/2) the generic Reconciler abstraction as a fix for this: the five restart-recovery bodies share nothing but a verb (Fixer.RecoverOrphanedFixes even lives in cmd/captain, separate binary). The CONCRETE missing piece stands: a ContractWorkerReconciler doing startup convergence for in-flight worker containers, alongside AssignmentReconciler + StateRecoveryManager. Keep scope concrete.
Fix wave launched (workflow: gobot-fix-ship-reclaim). Scope: contract fleet coordinator reclaims ships from FAILED/worker_interrupted child containers; gas/mfg exposure investigate-and-report only.
close_reason: Fixed+committed: ReclaimShipsFromInterruptedWorkers in the starvation branch (fires startup + periodic re-check, survives recovery-ordering race); RED-first test through the Handle port + foreign-container guard; existing spawn/recovery pins unchanged; gate green 230 tests. Gas not exposed (pre-release on assign); mfg covered by StateRecoveryManager.

Deliverable: ranked, adversarially-judged opportunity list; mikado prerequisite sketches for the big ones; filed as beads for Admiral triage. Input: crafters' skipped_for_l5l6 + suspected_bugs from workflow 1.

title: harbormaster
Verify 2 narrow residuals of interrupted-worker ship reclaim
description: Both found+recorded (not fixed) during the st-anu fix wave: (1) mfg carve-out UNVERIFIED: EXECUTING COLLECT_SELL/LIQUIDATE tasks preserve a dead-container ship assignment on recovery, and respawn uses ClaimShip (api/ship_repository.go ~:898) which rejects ships assigned to a different container unless the same container ID is reused - could strand a ship in the same way st-anu did for contracts; needs a targeted test or trace. (2) contract reclaim triggers only at FULL starvation (availableShips==0): ships pinned to dead workers while other haulers are idle stay pinned until starvation - acceptable within the original bug, but a partial-fleet-degradation window exists (reviewer MINOR). Both are small; fold into the next quality pass or captain observation once fleet runs.
notes: Admiral: yes to verification. upv-verifier agent dispatched (read-only trace of the EXECUTING COLLECT_SELL/LIQUIDATE preserved-assignment respawn path vs ClaimShip strictness). Verdict pending.
close_reason: Verified benign (upv-verifier, evidence chain file:line): residual 1 SELF-HEALS - carve-out preserves task.assignedShip only; daemon Start() runs ReleaseAllActive synchronously BEFORE RecoverRunningContainers goroutine exists, so respawn's ClaimShip(NEW id) always meets an idle ship (ship_repository.go:898 guard false -> idle-assign :907). All 3 dispatch callers omit ContainerID. Residual 2 (contract reclaim fires only at full starvation) stays known-bounded; promote only if the live fleet shows partial-degradation. HARDENING CANDIDATE (recorded, not filed): ReleaseAllActive failure is warn-only (daemon_server.go:272) - on failure a cargo-laden ship stays stuck until next restart while the task self-heals with a fresh ship; defense-in-depth = retry or fail-fast on that call.

title: Test-net promotion pass after structural round
description: Audit all pinning/characterization tests accumulated across the refactor waves (correctness wave, cleanup batch, structural parts 1-3). Per test: KEEP (already spec-grade, e.g. retry contract tests), PROMOTE (rename/reframe to assert intent), or DECIDE (quirk-pins encoding arguably-wrong behavior -> bless or fix+flip). Known quirk-pins to triage: RecordAPIRetry emits no retry metric when a single 429 precedes success (client_retry_test.go, preserved-not-fixed in part 1); #15 flight-mode exact-threshold asymmetry (intentional per e75752b but unprotected - needs its characterization test written). Use test-quality-auditor agent. Do NOT rewrite wholesale - coverage gaps beat rewrites; parts 2-3 prerequisites already add the missing recovery/spawn/table tests.
notes:
Admiral asked whether to replace char tests with real tests (2026-07-06). Verdict: promote+prune, not replace; blocked until part 3 lands so it runs once over the full set.
Split into phases per Admiral: A=inventory+classify (read-only, 36 test files from session commits c311ad1..6bded3c), B=mechanical promotions, C=quirk decisions at Admiral checkpoint + missing #15 test. Phase A launched.
Phase A done: 122 entries - 87 KEEP / 24 PROMOTE / 11 DECIDE (7 distinct decisions), 6 missing-test gaps (flight-mode #15 mandatory). Full manifest in workflow wf_b74b0a69-8cf output. Phase B (mechanical) launching; Phase C decisions presented to Admiral at checkpoint.
Phase B COMMITTED (8 files, 33+/33- exactly symmetric): 24/24 worklist entries applied, 10 sub-actions correctly skipped under no-new-comment rule / no label slots, assertions proven byte-identical by fable verifier, gate green. Phase C awaiting Admiral ruling on the 7 decisions presented at checkpoint.
Admiral: GO on all 7 Phase C decisions. Launching 7-lane parallel workflow: D1 retry-metric per-retry emission (api), D2 gas_siphon_worker registry alignment + recovery test (grpc), D3 spawn orphan-stop x3 paths (gas+contract), D4 purchaser matrix delete (mfg), D5 buyer-score unification per st-67r candidate 1 (persistence), D6 white-box assertion drop + cargo sync gap tests (contract/services), D7 flight-mode #15 + ledger first-transaction tests (domain+ledger).
close_reason: All 3 phases complete+committed. A: 122 tests audited (87 KEEP/24 PROMOTE/11 DECIDE). B: 24 mechanical promotions, assertions byte-identical. C: 4 approved behavior fixes RED-first (retry metrics per-retry, gas_siphon_worker alignment, spawn orphan-stop x3, buyer-score WEAK-best), 1 approved deletion correctly REFUSED on re-verification (purchaser not a delegate), 6 gap tests written incl mandatory flight-mode #15. Gates green throughout.

title: Orbit/Dock/tactics handlers duplicate the same load-ship + ensure-state + call-API skeleton
description:
Level L5 · effort S · analyzer value 3 · judge-adjusted 2 (2/2 real, 2/2 behavior-safe)

FILES: internal/application/ship/commands/tactics/orbit_ship.go, internal/application/ship/commands/tactics/dock_ship.go, internal/application/ship/commands/tactics/refuel_ship.go, internal/application/ship/commands/navigation/set_flight_mode.go, internal/application/ship/commands/navigation/navigate_direct.go, internal/application/ship/types/commands.go

PROBLEM: orbit_ship.go and dock_ship.go are structurally identical, differing only in EnsureInOrbit vs EnsureDocked, shipRepo.Orbit vs Dock, and status strings. The loadShip helper (cmd.Ship!=nil then FindBySymbol fallback) is copy-pasted verbatim across orbit, dock, refuel, set_flight_mode, and navigate_direct — 5+ identical copies. The cargo package already solved its equivalent duplication via CargoTransactionStrategy; tactics never got the treatment.

PROPOSAL: Add a ShipCommand accessor interface (GetShip/GetShipSymbol/GetPlayerID) plus one shared loadShip(ctx, repo, cmd). For orbit/dock, a tiny StateTransition strategy (ensureFn, apiFn, doneStatus, alreadyStatus) driving one shared handler, mirroring CargoTransactionStrategy. First step: extract shared loadShip and delete the 5 copies.

TEST PREREQ: Characterization test for the already-in-orbit/already-docked (stateChanged==false) branch so the no-op-API path is preserved.

JUDGE NOTES (condensed): Verified: orbit_ship.go/dock_ship.go are structurally identical and loadShip is copied verbatim 5x (7 loadShip funcs total); CargoTransactionStrategy precedent exists. So the problem is real and the extraction is mechanically behavior-safe with the stated characterization test. But value is low for this mission: (1) the loadShip accessor-interface approach partly relocates rather than eliminates — adding 3 accessor methods to 5-7 command structs (~15-21 methods) to delete ~50 lines of a benign, non-diverging 10-line helper is a near line-count wash, only centralizing change points; an embedded base struct would eliminate more cleanly but wasn't chosen. (2) The orbit/dock strategy adds indirection to dead-simple hot-path handlers with distinctly-typed responses needing a response factory. (3) Coverage is nearly absent — the tactics package has zero test files — so the single prereq test is a thin net for restructuring 5 handlers; refuel (ledger/metrics) and navigate_direct (4204 reconcile) carry more logic and should be excluded. In a live 150k/hr, gate-priority operation with thin tests, adding abstraction to constantly-exercised ship-movement handlers raises incident-time tracing
close_reason: Closed

title: Move endpoint-name classification out of the API transport layer
description:
Level L6 · effort S · analyzer value 3 · judge-adjusted 2 (2/2 real, 2/2 behavior-safe)

FILES: internal/adapters/api/client.go

PROBLEM: client.go carries ~160 lines of pure metrics-labeling concern: extractEndpoint, endpointToHumanName (rebuilds a 40-entry pattern->name map on EVERY request — per-request allocation on the hot path of every API call), normalizePath, isDynamicSegment, and the looksLikeShipSymbol/System/Waypoint/ContractID/FactionSymbol heuristics. Path taxonomy, not HTTP transport.

PROPOSAL: Extract an endpointClassifier type with the nameMap hoisted to a package-level var built once; request() calls classifier.HumanName(path)/Endpoint(path). First step: hoist nameMap to a package var and move the five looksLike* + normalizePath funcs into endpoint_classifier.go.

TEST PREREQ: none

JUDGE NOTES (condensed): Verified in client.go: the five looksLike* heuristics, normalizePath, isDynamicSegment, extractEndpoint, and endpointToHumanName (lines 1392-1555, ~163 lines) are pure path-classification functions with no HTTP/client-state dependency. The per-request allocation is real: endpointToHumanName rebuilds the 40-entry nameMap literal (line 1419) on every call, and request() invokes extractEndpoint once per API call (line 1568). Hoisting the read-only map to a package var genuinely eliminates the alloc, not relocates it. Behavior-safe: pure funcs moved intra-package + a read-only literal promoted to package var is mechanical and preserves behavior; no test prereq required. However the value is low: the 'hot path per-request allocation' framing is overstated (a 40-entry map build is microseconds, and the real path at ~42 req/sec is gated by the rate limiter/network at line 1574, not this alloc). It is an intra-package file split with no actual dependency decoupling; the one concrete win is a 2-line map hoist wrapped in a larger cosmetic move. Against a gate-priority mission with thin tests, this is marginal — adjusted down from 3 to 2. ||| Verified: client.go lines 1392-1555 (~164 of 1984
close_reason: Closed

title: Replace drift-prone pipeline task counters with computed queries
description:
Level L5 · effort S · analyzer value 3 · judge-adjusted 2.5 (2/2 real, 2/2 behavior-safe)

FILES: internal/domain/manufacturing/pipeline.go

PROBLEM: ManufacturingPipeline caches taskCount/tasksReady/tasksDone/tasksFailed (pipeline.go:76-79) that drift by design: Start() increments tasksReady inline; recalculateTaskStats() is only invoked from SetTasks(); nothing updates tasksDone/tasksFailed at runtime, so Progress() reports stale 0% on any live pipeline. TasksReady()/TasksDone()/TasksFailed() have zero production consumers outside the domain — the counters are pure liability.

PROPOSAL: Delete the four counter fields; make TaskCount()=len(p.tasks) and TasksReady/TasksDone/TasksFailed/Progress iterate p.tasks on demand. Delete recalculateTaskStats and the inline tasksReady++ in Start(). First step: characterization test that Progress() after Start()+task.Complete() currently returns 0, then flip to computed and assert correct value.

TEST PREREQ: Characterization test of Progress()/GetReadyTasks() across Start -> assign -> complete lifecycle, and of the ReconstitutePipeline+SetTasks path used by the repository.

JUDGE NOTES (condensed): Verified in pipeline.go: tasksDone/tasksFailed are written only by recalculateTaskStats(), called only from SetTasks() (line 295); nothing updates them when a task completes at runtime, and Start() does inline tasksReady++ (line 343). So Progress() (tasksDone/taskCount, line 427) returns a stale 0 on any pipeline built via NewPipeline->AddTask->Start. Drift is real and by design. grep confirms TasksReady/Done/Failed have zero production consumers, and pipeline Progress() is consumed only by String() (line 451) (the container_ops Progress calls are mat/site types, not pipeline). Replacing the cached fields with on-demand iteration eliminates the invalidation obligation rather than relocating it. Behavior-safe given the stated characterization tests: the only observable change is the unused getters + Progress() returning correct instead of stale values, invisible to production. Two constraints the executor must honor: keep TaskCount() = len(p.tasks) since it HAS production consumers (container_ops_construction.go, construction_pipeline_planner.go), and preserve the !task.CanRetry() guard in the computed TasksFailed. adjusted_value is modest (2): clean low-risk self-contained cleanup
close_reason: Closed

title: Remove speculative dead domain services: TaskPriorityCalculator, LiquidationPolicy, unused Task
description:
Level L6 · effort S · analyzer value 3 · judge-adjusted 3 (2/2 real, 2/2 behavior-safe)

FILES: internal/domain/manufacturing/priority_calculator.go, internal/domain/manufacturing/liquidation_policy.go, internal/domain/manufacturing/task_readiness_spec.go, internal/application/manufacturing/commands/run_factory_worker.go, internal/application/manufacturing/services/manufacturing/market_condition_checker.go

PROBLEM: Four pieces of speculative scaffolding: TaskPriorityCalculator has a constructor and clock field but ZERO methods (real priority logic lives in effectivePriority() in task_queue.go); LiquidationPolicy is an empty struct never instantiated; TaskReadinessSpecification.CanExecute/EvaluateReadiness/CanSourceFromMarket have zero production callers and CanExecute silently returns false for STORAGE_ACQUIRE_DELIVER and DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION — a latent trap; RunFactoryWorkerHandler (161 lines) is registered with no mediator and referenced by no factory registry.

PROPOSAL: Delete priority_calculator.go, liquidation_policy.go, run_factory_worker.go (and WorkerResult plumbing), drop the readinessSpec field from MarketConditionChecker, and trim TaskReadinessSpecification to what has callers. First step: delete the two empty domain shells — pure win, zero behavior change.

TEST PREREQ: none

JUDGE NOTES (condensed): Verified against current code. All four items are genuinely dead: (1) TaskPriorityCalculator is a struct+constructor with zero methods and zero external callers; real priority logic is effectivePriority() in task_queue.go:324. (2) LiquidationPolicy is an empty struct with zero references. (3) RunFactoryWorkerHandler (161 lines) has zero callers, no mediator/registry registration, no test refs; its command/WorkerResult types are used only within the file. (4) TaskReadinessSpecification's methods CanExecute/EvaluateReadiness/CanSourceFromMarket have zero callers (grep empty); the struct is instantiated and threaded into MarketConditionChecker.readinessSpec but that field is stored and never read, so 'trim to what has callers' resolves to full deletion plus dropping the unused field/arg at the single caller (coordinator:366). The latent trap is real (CanExecute default-returns false for the actual task types STORAGE_ACQUIRE_DELIVER and DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION) but currently inert. Behavior-safe: all deletions have zero live callers, no tests reference any of it, and the only wiring edits are compiler-checked — thin tests are irrelevant since there is no behavior to preserve. Value cap
close_reason: Closed

title: ISP: split the 12-method ManufacturingTaskQueue interface consumed 1-3 methods at a time
description:
Level L6 · effort S · analyzer value 3 · judge-adjusted 2 (2/2 real, 2/2 behavior-safe)

FILES: internal/application/manufacturing/services/task_queue.go, internal/application/manufacturing/services/manufacturing/pipeline_recycler.go, internal/application/manufacturing/services/manufacturing/task_rescuer.go, internal/application/manufacturing/services/manufacturing/factory_state_manager.go, internal/application/manufacturing/services/manufacturing/worker_lifecycle_manager.go, internal/application/manufacturing/services/manufacturing/task_assignment_manager.go, internal/application/manufacturing/services/manufacturing/state_recovery_manager.go, internal/application/manufacturing/services/manufacturing/pipeline_lifecycle_manager.go

PROBLEM: ManufacturingTaskQueue (task_queue.go:14-50) exposes 12 methods but measured per-consumer usage is 1-3 methods (pipeline_recycler uses only Remove; factory_state_manager and pipeline_lifecycle_manager only Enqueue; etc.). Every test fake must stub 12 methods, and the fat interface is the main reason 7 files in the nested services/manufacturing package import the parent services package — the coupling that blocks the package-cohesion dissolve.

PROPOSAL: Define consumer-side narrow interfaces: TaskEnqueuer {Enqueue}, TaskRemover {Remove}, ReadyTaskReader {GetReadyTasks, HasReadyTasksByType}; take those in each constructor; keep the full interface only for coordinator wiring. First step: change pipeline_recycler and factory_state_manager (single-method consumers) — purely mechanical, compiler-verified. Unlocks the package dissolve item.

TEST PREREQ: none

JUDGE NOTES (condensed): Verified in code. The ISP violation is real: ManufacturingTaskQueue exposes 12 methods (task_queue.go:14-50) while each consumer uses 1-3 (pipeline_recycler=Remove only; factory_state_manager/state_recovery_manager/pipeline_lifecycle_manager=Enqueue only; task_rescuer/worker_lifecycle_manager=2; task_assignment_manager=3). Consumer-side narrow interfaces are the standard Go idiom and genuinely eliminate (not relocate) the coupling: for 6 of the 7 files, services.ManufacturingTaskQueue is the ONLY reference to the parent services package, so narrowing lets them drop the import. The first step (pipeline_recycler + factory_state_manager, single-method, sole-coupling) is purely mechanical, compiler-verified, and behavior-preserving since the concrete *TaskQueue already satisfies any narrow interface; empty test_prereq is adequate. HOWEVER two justifications are inflated: (1) 'Every test fake must stub 12 methods' is FALSE — no fake of this interface exists anywhere in internal/; the only consumer test uses the real services.NewTaskQueue(). (2) 'Unlocks the package dissolve' is overstated — pipeline_lifecycle_manager also imports ManufacturingDemandFinder, CollectionOpportunityFinder, P
close_reason: Closed

title: Delete dead config package (duplicate supply-chain table, zero importers)
description:
Level L6 · effort S · analyzer value 3 · judge-adjusted 3 (2/2 real, 2/2 behavior-safe)

FILES: internal/application/manufacturing/config/factory_config.go

PROBLEM: The entire package is imported by NOTHING (verified by grep). Its 177 lines include DefaultSupplyChainMap(), a hand-maintained copy of a subset of internal/domain/goods/supply_chain.go. Two supply-chain truth tables can silently diverge, and env-var config documented here (GOODS_POLL_INTERVAL_*, GOODS_PARALLEL_ENABLED) is never read, misleading operators.

PROPOSAL: Delete internal/application/manufacturing/config entirely. If any env-var knobs are wanted, they belong on the live RunParallelManufacturingCoordinatorCommand defaults. First step: git rm the package, run go build ./....

TEST PREREQ: none

JUDGE NOTES (condensed): Verified against current code. (1) real: The package internal/application/manufacturing/config is imported by NOTHING — `grep -rn "application/manufacturing/config"` returns zero hits (the LoadConfig/FactoryConfig grep noise is a distinct package, internal/infrastructure/config). The file (177 lines, confirmed) contains DefaultSupplyChainMap(), a hand-maintained subset-copy of the supply-chain table, and documents GOODS_POLL_INTERVAL_*/GOODS_PARALLEL_ENABLED which are read nowhere else in the codebase. Deletion eliminates the divergence trap and misleading docs outright — it does not relocate them (no env-var knobs are being moved anywhere; the proposal's "if wanted, put on live command" is a future non-commitment). (2) behavior_safe: with the file moved out, `go build ./...` returns RC=0. Since nothing imports it, no runtime behavior changes and thin tests are irrelevant; empty test_prereq is acceptable. (3) adjusted_value 3: a clean, genuinely-dead-code L6 delete with near-zero risk; value is real but modest for a 150k/hr gate-priority operation since it touches nothing on the hot path — its worth is preventing a second supply-chain truth table from silently diverging and removin
close_reason: Closed

Admiral to decide; until then city config has no backup/history. Also untracked: the depth-3 supply-filter bug report (tracked in st-nvl).

title: Dead registry entry: gas_transport_worker (no producer, no ContainerKind)
description: Phase C discovery (grpc lane): the ContainerSpec registry carries gas_transport_worker but no production code persists that command type and no ContainerKind exists for it in domain/daemon/ports.go. Either a never-shipped worker type (delete the entry) or a planned one (add the kind when built). One-line check+delete for a future cleanup batch.

title: Decide: contradictory activity-ordering tables - unify or bless (2 pinned candidates)
description: RESOLVED candidate 1 per Admiral (2026-07-06): buyer-side paths unify on BuyerActivityScore (WEAK best) - market_repository scoreMarketForBuying flipped as explicit diff in test-promotion Phase C. REMAINING SCOPE candidate 2 only: manufacturing_opportunity 4th unique ordering (WEAK>RESTRICTED>STRONG>GROWING) stays pinned+local - intent murkier, low stakes until fleet trades; captain may revisit with live market data.
notes:
Candidate 1 EXECUTED in phase C: scoreMarketForBuying unified on BuyerActivityScore, pins flipped RED-first. Side effect (intended): unknown activity now ordinal 2 (domain default = STRONG-equivalent) instead of worst. Candidate 2 (manufacturing_opportunity 4th ordering) remains the bead's only open scope.
Admiral ruling: hold candidate 2 for live market data - the captain rules on manufacturing_opportunity's ordering with evidence once the new universe trades. Bead stays open as a captain-era task; no dry-dock action.

title: L5-L6 below-the-line: 28 unjudged opportunities (future judging round)
description:
28 opportunities ranked by synthesis but NOT adversarially judged (budget line at top-12). Do not treat as endorsed - judge before executing. Several v4 candidates worth a future judging round:

- [L6/S/v2] Dissolve pkg/utils and normalize duplicate getters
- [L5/L/v4] SupplyLevel/ActivityLevel domain value objects + MarketScoringPolicy: retire 10+ contradictory supply/activity tables across all four layers
- [L5/M/v4] Consolidate three divergent ship-JSON mappers in the API client
- [L5/M/v4] Extract triplicated persist->transfer-ship->start-with-rollback worker-spawn orchestration
- [L5/L/v4] Single command-factory source of truth for container launch AND recovery, with typed config accessors (kills the restart-orphan bug class)
- [L5/M/v4] Extract a retry/backoff policy from the request() mega-method
- [L6/M/v3] Delete-or-implement the stub HealthMonitor (fake recovery metrics, zero consumers)
- [L6/M/v3] Split fat PipelineManager interface and remove the coordinator's downcast to the concrete manager
- [L6/M/v3] Two divergent composition roots register the same mediator handlers inconsistently
- [L5/M/v3] Consolidate scattered switch-on-TaskType chains into a TaskType descriptor
- [L5/M/v3] Collapse four duplicate lifecycle status enums and fix GoodsFactory dual state machine
- [L6/M/v3] Evict manufacturing/gas coordination events from navigation's ShipEventPublisher/Subscriber
- [L6/M/v3] ISP sweep: split the 27-method ports.APIClient god interface into per-domain role interfaces
- [L5/M/v3] Extract shared cargo-acquisition phase from the four task executors
- [L5/M/v3] Untyped mediator RequestHandler forces 52 copies of type-assert boilerplate
- [L6/M/v3] Move coordinator service graph construction out of the handler into the composition root
- [L5/M/v3] Enforce StorageShip capacity invariant with a Reservation ticket value object
- [L6/M/v2] DIP: application layer calls adapters/metrics package-level globals directly
- [L6/M/v2] Logging-discipline sweep: 108 fmt.Printf calls in daemon/adapter/application production paths bypass the structured logger
- [L6/L/v4] SRP split of the SupplyMonitor god type (28 methods, ~1570 lines, 10-param ctor)
- [L6/L/v4] Collapse fat DaemonClient interface: per-worker-type Persist/Start method pairs violate ISP+OCP
- [L6/L/v3] Dissolve the stuttering services/manufacturing sub-package into execution + orchestration packages
- [L6/L/v3] Split the 20-method ShipRepository god interface into role interfaces (ISP)
- [L6/L/v3] Split the ShipRepository god type; extract a dedicated ship mapper
- [L6/L/v3] Split construction-variant fields out of the ManufacturingPipeline god aggregate
- [L5/M/v2] Coordinator commands thread PlayerID+ContainerID+CoordinatorID as loose params (deprecated fields linger)
- [L6/M/v2] application/common is a deprecated re-export hub coupling 93 files to 5 packages
close_reason: The 7 v4 heavyweights all executed via st-ege parts 1-3; remaining 21 below-line items stay recorded in docs/refactoring/2026-07-05-l5l6-discovery.json for future rounds

title: Cosmetic: bd prefix 'st' vs HQ rig prefix 'sp'
description: City bd issues use prefix st; rig registry rendered spacetraders HQ prefix as sp. No data conflict yet - unify only if it bothers anyone.
close_reason: Resolved for real during cutover: rig db now genuinely mints sp- (issue_prefix fixed post bd-init quirk, saved to bd memories); city st-, rig sp- - the split the spec wanted.

value: Admiral model preference: never use haiku for workflow agents, even mechanical steps - use sonnet as the floor. Better: avoid LLM-as-file-reader entirely; pass data inline via workflow args (scripts lack fs access, but the orchestrator can embed the data).
